label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the given text. The author starts with "Do you start your work by making a list..." followed by several questions about list-making habits. So, they're asking the reader if they do various things related to making to-do lists. Then, the author states that if you are a natural list maker, you can consider this habit as a good thing. After this introduction, the text lists several reasons (gives your memory a kick, helps you stay in the moment, etc.) as benefits of making to-do lists. The question is asking how the author introduces the topic. The options are: (A) asking a series of questions (B) presenting some simple facts (C) stating his own point of view (D) listing the benefits of a to-do list The correct answer is (A)
